comment_text.sn1.36:1.3: The Pali text attributes none of the verses to the Buddha. However, in the two Chinese parallels at SA 1286 and SA-2 284, one deity gives an opening verse; the Buddha responds with a series of 5 or 6 verses; then the deity concludes with a verse in praise. This gives a much more satisfying structure, and it seems likely the Pali text has suffered loss.
comment_text.sn1.36:2.1: This sounds like a typical deity verse in that it culminates by going to heaven, but it is attributed to the Buddha in the Chinese parallels.
comment_text.sn1.36:4.1: This verse sounds unlike something a deity would say, and like something the Buddha would say.
comment_text.sn1.36:4.4: While the deity is said to recite several verses, the Pali text finishes here with a close -ti indicating the end of the quote.
comment_text.sn1.36:5.1: The Pali text indicates no speaker for the final verses, but presumably they too were spoken by the Buddha.

root_text.sn1.36:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn1.36:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā satullapakāyikā devatāyo abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.sn1.36:1.3: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho ekā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.36:2.3: Yaso ca kittī ca tatvassa hoti,
root_text.sn1.36:3.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn1.36:0.2: 4. The Host of a Hundred Felicities
translation_text.sn1.36:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn1.36:1.2: Then, late at night, several glorious deities of the host of a hundred felicities, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side.
translation_text.sn1.36:1.3: Standing to one side, one deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.36:2.4: and when they discard this corpse they go to heaven.”
translation_text.sn1.36:3.1: Then another deity recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.36:4.1: “Give up anger, get rid of conceit,
translation_text.sn1.36:4.2: and get past all the fetters.
translation_text.sn1.36:4.3: Chains don’t torment one who has nothing,
translation_text.sn1.36:4.4: not clinging to name and form.”
translation_text.sn1.36:6.3: For if you’re diligent and practice absorption,
